title: Cell Structure & Organelles
content: The cell is the basic structural unit of all living organisms. Eukaryotic cells contain membrane-bound organelles like mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, and the nucleus. Prokaryotic cells lack these structures.
excerpt: The cell is the basic structural unit of all living organisms...

title: Chain Rule Explained
content: The chain rule is used to differentiate composite functions. If y = f(g(x)), then dy/dx = f'(g(x)) * g'(x). This is fundamental for solving complex derivative problems.
excerpt: The chain rule is used to differentiate composite functions...

title: Causes of the French Revolution
content: Economic hardship, social inequality, and Enlightenment ideas sparked the French Revolution. The ancien régime's financial crisis, combined with famine and Enlightenment philosophy, created perfect conditions for revolution.
excerpt: Economic hardship, social inequality and Enlightenment ideas...

title: Photosynthesis Process
content: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy through the light-dependent and light-independent reactions. Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll which captures light energy.
excerpt: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy through reactions...

title: Chemical Bonding Types
content: There are three main types of chemical bonds: ionic (between metals and nonmetals), covalent (between nonmetals), and metallic (between metals). Each has distinct properties.
excerpt: Three main types of chemical bonds: ionic, covalent, and metallic...

title: Integration by Parts
content: Integration by parts uses the formula: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du. This technique helps integrate products of functions. Proper choice of u and dv is crucial.
excerpt: Integration by parts formula and techniques for solving complex integrals...

title: Industrial Revolution Timeline
content: The Industrial Revolution (1760-1840) began in Britain with mechanization of textile production. It spread globally, transforming economies, societies, and technology.
excerpt: The Industrial Revolution transformed economies and societies globally...

title: Periodic Table Organization
content: Elements are organized by atomic number and electron configuration. Periods are rows, groups are columns. Similar properties appear in the same group.
excerpt: Elements organized by atomic number and electron configuration...
